claim:
The patient was seen in the Emergency
Department and was found to have electrocardiogram findings
of ST elevations in I, L and V6, as well as deep T wave
inversions inferiorly.
evidence_preview: T wave inversions in leads II, III and aVF.

claim:
He became hypotensive after two
sublingual nitroglycerin, and transiently bradycardic.
evidence_preview: Pt recv'g 1SL NTG and 1mg of MSO4 and becomeing hypotensive into the 80s and BP rebounding on own.

claim:
He
was therefore referred for a cardiac catheterization.
evidence_preview:
CCU NPN
s: "I was so sick last night"
O: see carevue for all objective data
cv: hr 70's sr w/ occ pvc.

claim:
In the catheterization laboratory, he was found to have an
left anterior descending with mild disease but a high
diagonal with a 90% occlusion.
evidence_preview: In Cath Lab found to have TO RCA, CIRC, and Diag.

claim:
He also had a total occlusion
of his right coronary artery and left circumflex, with
collateralization.
evidence_preview: Coronary artery disease.

claim:
The diagonal was intervened upon and
stented.
evidence_preview: DIAG stented.

claim:
He was transferred to the Coronary Care Unit
post-procedure for monitoring.
evidence_preview: Coronary artery disease.

claim:

hypercholesterolemia, coronary artery disease status post
myocardial infarction in [**2192**] with percutaneous transluminal
coronary angioplasty of his right coronary artery, history of
guaiac positive stools after receiving aspirin, depression,
anxiety.
evidence_preview: Coronary artery disease.

claim:

Glucotrol, Prozac 20 mg once daily, Xanax .5 mg four times a
day as needed.

claim:

evidence_preview: ST segment elevations in I, aVL and V2-V3.

claim:
